extraction.one_line_summary: BEVIN Patrick won with a late-race acceleration in the final 400m after a reduced bunch sprint following multiple late attacks on the climb of Sidera.
tactical_patterns: VERONA Carlos attacked on flat sections of the penultimate climb rather than steep gradients, forcing disorganized counterattacks.
DENNIS Rohan defended leader jersey by surviving reduced sprint rather than controlling breakaway, conserving team resources for queen stage.
TAARAMÄE Rein launched solo descent-to-finish move with 500m remaining, exploiting hesitation after bunch regrouped from penultimate climb.
Israel Premier Tech prioritized KOM points collection via breakaway rather than stage win setup, yet BEVIN won from reduced peloton.

rider_signals.BEVIN Patrick: positive: delivered explosive final 400m acceleration from reduced group, suggesting hidden sprint power after hilly courses
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: retained jersey with third place and time bonus despite leading team's workload, showing form depth before queen stage
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: neutral: initiated counterattacks on Sidera but couldn't sustain separation, testing legs before decisive mountain stage
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: neutral: followed MÄDER attack but regrouped on descent, reconnaissance riding rather than genuine GC move
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: second place from reduced sprint after climbs indicates strong hilly-sprint versatility
rider_signals.VERONA Carlos: neutral: attacked penultimate climb strategically but lacked support to hold 9-second gap over flat approach
